title: Observe Your Own Mind
description: Apply psychological concepts to your own thoughts, emotions, and behavior through structured self-observation.

steps.title: Catch a cognitive bias in action
steps.instruction: Over the next 24 hours, try to catch yourself using an availability heuristic or confirmation bias.
steps.prompt: Describe a moment when you made a judgment based on what was most easily available to your memory. Was it accurate? What information were you missing?

steps.title: Map an emotion
steps.instruction: Pick an emotion you felt today. Use the valence-arousal model: Where is it on the pleasant/unpleasant axis? High or low arousal?
steps.prompt: Name the emotion as specifically as you can. What triggered it? What action tendency did it create (approach, avoid, freeze, connect)?

steps.title: Identify your motivation type
steps.instruction: Think of something you are currently working on or toward. Is your motivation primarily intrinsic or extrinsic?
steps.prompt: Describe the goal. What drives you to pursue it? How would removing external rewards affect your motivation?

steps.title: Trace an attribution
steps.instruction: Think of something that went wrong recently. Did you attribute it to yourself (internal) or to circumstances (external)?
steps.prompt: Write the attribution you made. Is there a self-serving bias here? What would a more balanced attribution look like?

steps.title: Identify a skill on your acquisition curve
steps.instruction: Pick a skill you are currently learning. Which stage are you in: cognitive (understanding), associative (reducing errors), or autonomous (automatic)?
steps.prompt: Describe where you are in skill acquisition. What would move you to the next stage?
